_description: Standing macro/geopolitical situations referenced across briefings. Loader: scripts/generate_daily.py:load_ongoing_situations(). Manually maintained. Set status to 'resolved' when a situation is no longer relevant; the loader will skip it. Add new situations when they enter the briefing rotation. Keep facts list under 6 items per situation (loader caps at 6). Anchor phrase is the one-clause framing used on FIRST reference each week per Rule 18.

iran_hormuz.anchor: Iran-Hormuz tensions, with the Strait of Hormuz premium that built since early April now deflating on diplomatic progress
facts: five oil and gas carriers cleared the Strait the week of May 25 (three oil tankers, two LNG)
U.S.-Iran diplomatic channels reportedly opened via Swiss intermediaries
crude lost roughly 19% in May as the premium unwinds
fresh U.S. strikes remained limited to military targets, did not escalate the broader conflict
shipping lanes remain navigable, premium is shrinking not gone

china_trade_deal.anchor: China's $17 billion annual US ag purchase commitment through 2028, announced May 18
facts: commitment spans beans, beef, and poultry beyond existing soybean commitments
North Dakota State study estimates active China tariffs cost US ag $15B annually, partially offsetting the commitment
promises have not yet translated into sustained futures support past the initial bounce
Senate ratification path uncertain

cargill_fort_morgan_lockout.anchor: the ongoing Cargill Fort Morgan/Schuyler plant lockout that began Tuesday May 19
facts: 1,700 workers locked out, processing chain disrupted at multiple plants
plant processes roughly 6,000 head daily, removing ~2% of weekly US slaughter capacity
drove the cattle complex split: feeders dropped 2.5% on May 22, live cattle held; then live broke $242 on May 29
no resolution announced as of May 29; chart damage already in feeder and live cattle prices
creating processing-constrained price dynamics rather than supply-constrained

planting_season_2026.anchor: the 2026 planting season, behind the average through May before catching up between rain systems
facts: corn planting reached 42% as of week ending May 24 per USDA Crop Progress
Missouri and Iowa farmers caught dry windows between rain systems to push progress
Belt running ahead of 5-year average despite wet May start
soil moisture levels running 150% of normal in key districts
prevent-plant deadlines cluster late May through mid-June by state
no weather premium being priced until prevent-plant deadlines approach
